# Implementation Plan: UI Test Duplicate Cleanup

**Implementation Branch**: `main` (feature label: `005-ui-test-cleanup`) | **Date**: 2026-06-27 | **Spec**: [spec.md](spec.md)

**Input**: Feature specification from `specs/005-ui-test-cleanup/spec.md`

**Note**: Generated by the `/speckit.plan` workflow. This plan refactors the required UI test files only, introduces shared UI test helpers, and preserves behavior-equivalent coverage without changing user-facing product behavior.

## Summary

Refactor duplicated NextPaste UI test code into a shared XCTest helper layer composed of a base UI test case, fixtures, Robot helpers for history, clipboard, and row actions, plus common assertions. The required refactoring scope is limited to `HistoryListUITests.swift`, `ClipboardAutoCaptureUITests.swift`, `ClipRowActionsUITests.swift`, and `VisualIdentityUITests.swift`. The refactor keeps the app behavior unchanged, allows only non-user-facing UI-testing-gated production hooks if unavoidable, and treats Sonar duplicate-line reduction as a hard completion gate.

## Technical Context

**Language/Version**: Swift UI tests in the existing Xcode project. The project currently uses Swift language mode `5.0` in checked-in project settings and is built with the repository's current Xcode SDKs.

**Primary Dependencies**: XCTest/XCUITest for UI automation, AppKit `NSPasteboard` behind `#if os(macOS)` for clipboard fixtures, Foundation for timing and strings, and the existing `UITestAppLauncher` for UI testing launch arguments and window management. No new third-party dependencies are introduced.

**Storage**: No new production or test persistence. Tests continue to use the existing `-ui-testing` local test store and the system pasteboard for clipboard simulation. Shared fixtures are in-memory constants only.

**Testing**: XCTest UI automation in `NextPasteUITests`. Validation uses focused Xcode UI test runs for the four required scenario classes, then the full `NextPasteUITests` target and optionally the full app test suite. Duplicate-line validation uses the project or CI Sonar report when available; otherwise a recorded manual duplicate-pattern comparison is required.

**Target Platform**: macOS UI test execution for this feature. Helper code must keep platform-specific pasteboard behavior behind compile-time checks and should not reduce the repository's broader Apple-platform build compatibility.

**Project Type**: Single Xcode SwiftUI app with one app target, one Swift Testing unit test target, and one XCTest UI test target.

**Performance Goals**: Performance is non-gating for this refactor because it changes UI test structure only; existing UI test timeouts remain the only timing bounds. Keep UI waits bounded to existing timeouts or shorter targeted waits, and do not add broad sleeps.

**Constraints**: Required scope is limited to four named UI test files. Production app code must remain unchanged unless a minimal non-user-facing testability hook is required and gated to UI testing. User-facing copy, layout, clipboard capture, duplicate handling, copy/delete/pin behavior, and visual identity behavior must not change. Sonar duplicate-code reduction is a hard completion gate. Assertions may be reorganized, but behavior-equivalent scenario intent and user-observable outcomes must remain covered.

**Scale/Scope**: Add shared helper files under `NextPasteUITests/`: `UITestCase.swift`, `HistoryRobot.swift`, `ClipboardRobot.swift`, `RowRobot.swift`, `UITestAssertions.swift`, and `UITestFixtures.swift`. Refactor only `HistoryListUITests.swift`, `ClipboardAutoCaptureUITests.swift`, `ClipRowActionsUITests.swift`, and `VisualIdentityUITests.swift` to consume those helpers. Existing helper `UITestAppLauncher.swift` remains available and may be reused rather than duplicated.

## Implementation Architecture

### Helper Layering

`UITestCase` becomes the base class for the four required scenario files. It centralizes `continueAfterFailure`, app launch variants, teardown registration, and access to Robot/helper entry points. It should wrap the existing `UITestAppLauncher` rather than replace it unless replacement is needed to remove duplication.

`UITestFixtures` owns deterministic clip values and derived expected values used by the refactored tests. It covers normal text clips, long multiline preview input and expected preview, automatic-capture values, duplicate/blank clipboard values, copy-failure text, pin/delete companions, local-only row action text, and visual identity text. Fixtures must stay local to the UI test target and must not represent production data.

`HistoryRobot` owns user-visible history operations: creating clips through the New Clip UI, waiting for the history list and surface, locating rows by text or preview, counting clip rows, and comparing vertical row order. It should hide repeated button, editor, list, and row-identifier queries.

`ClipboardRobot` owns pasteboard interactions and capture-oriented app state transitions: setting and reading clipboard text where supported, waiting for auto-captured rows, backgrounding the app, minimizing the app, reactivating the app, and reopening the main window. It should keep AppKit-specific code isolated behind `#if os(macOS)`.

`RowRobot` owns row-level interactions: selecting a row to copy, tapping explicit copy controls, revealing pin/delete actions using a bounded gesture retry loop, pinning, unpinning, deleting, and targeting the intended row when multiple clips exist. It must fail clearly if the expected row or action is not reachable.

`UITestAssertions` owns common assertions for accessible text, existence and absence, history state, row identifiers, row order, copied feedback appearance/disappearance, no copied feedback on failure, pinned icon state, deletion outcomes, toolbar controls, empty state copy/illustration, and visual identity surfaces. Assertion helpers should accept file/line parameters where practical so failures point to the scenario.

### Refactoring Strategy

1. Add helper files with no scenario behavior changes.
2. Move repeated setup, clip creation, clipboard access, row count, row action reveal, drag, wait-for-disappearance, accessible-text, row-order, and visual-state assertions into helpers.
3. Refactor `HistoryListUITests` to use fixtures, base launch, `HistoryRobot`, and shared assertions while preserving newest-first ordering, long multiline preview, history surface, single-column layout, row identifier, and full-text absence outcomes.
4. Refactor `ClipboardAutoCaptureUITests` to use base auto-capture launch, `ClipboardRobot`, `HistoryRobot`, `RowRobot`, and shared assertions while preserving foreground, background, minimized, duplicate/blank/unchanged, redesigned row path, copy, pin, and delete outcomes.
5. Refactor `ClipRowActionsUITests` to use shared launch, fixtures, `HistoryRobot`, `ClipboardRobot`, `RowRobot`, and shared assertions while preserving copy feedback, pasteboard value, accessibility labels, copy failure, delete-only-selected, pin ordering, local UI test store, and auto-captured offline row actions.
6. Refactor `VisualIdentityUITests` to use base launch, fixtures, `HistoryRobot`, and shared visual assertions while preserving warm canvas, single-column layout, history surface/list, absence of sidebar/detail, empty state copy/illustration, populated-row illustration absence, toolbar/search/filter/settings placeholder behavior, and absence of advanced/settings windows.
7. Add minimal production testability hooks only if an existing assertion cannot remain reliable through UI-only helpers; such hooks must be non-user-facing and gated by existing UI-testing launch arguments.
8. Validate behavior parity with focused UI tests, then validate duplicate reduction using Sonar evidence or the documented manual fallback.

## Constitution Check

*GATE: Must pass before Phase 0 research. Re-check after Phase 1 design.*

### Pre-Research Gate

- **Clipboard-first product**: PASS. The refactor preserves existing automatic capture coverage and explicitly verifies the clipboard-driven `Clipboard Changed -> Detect -> Validate -> Deduplicate -> Persist -> Refresh UI` behavior without changing product flow.
- **Local-first architecture**: PASS. The plan adds UI test helpers only. Tests continue to use local UI testing launch arguments, local SwiftData-backed behavior, and the system pasteboard; no remote storage or sync requirement is introduced.
- **Privacy by default**: PASS. Test clip values remain local to the UI test process and pasteboard. The plan adds no analytics, telemetry, Firebase, advertising SDKs, network reporting, or clipboard-data transmission.
- **Automatic capture**: PASS. Existing foregrounded, backgrounded, minimized, duplicate, blank, unchanged, and row-action coverage for auto-captured clips remains required.
- **Test-first coverage**: PASS. This feature is itself a test-suite refactor. The plan requires behavior-equivalent UI test coverage and focused validation before completion.
- **Native simplicity**: PASS. The implementation uses XCTest/XCUITest, AppKit where already required for macOS pasteboard behavior, Foundation, and existing project helpers only. No dependency or platform deviation is required.

### Post-Design Gate

- **Clipboard-first product**: PASS. The data model and contracts keep automatic capture and history assertions as first-class test flows and preserve clipboard-triggered user outcomes.
- **Local-first architecture**: PASS. Design artifacts define in-memory test helpers and local pasteboard interactions only; no persisted model, network, or CloudKit requirement is added.
- **Privacy by default**: PASS. Contracts prohibit telemetry, remote services, and clipboard transmission, and quickstart validation uses local Xcode/Sonar evidence only.
- **Automatic capture**: PASS. Contracts require foreground, background, minimized, duplicate, blank, unchanged, and auto-captured row action parity.
- **Test-first coverage**: PASS. Quickstart commands validate the focused UI classes and full UI test target, and Sonar/manual duplicate evidence is a completion gate.
- **Native simplicity**: PASS. The helper architecture stays inside `NextPasteUITests/` and reuses `UITestAppLauncher`; production hooks remain exceptional and UI-testing-gated.

## Project Structure

### Documentation (this feature)

```text
specs/005-ui-test-cleanup/
├── plan.md
├── research.md
├── data-model.md
├── quickstart.md
├── contracts/
│   ├── behavior-parity-contracts.md
│   └── ui-test-helper-contracts.md
└── tasks.md              # Created later by /speckit.tasks
```

### Source Code (repository root)

```text
NextPasteUITests/
├── UITestAppLauncher.swift                 # Existing launch/window helper, reused
├── UITestCase.swift                        # New shared base class
├── UITestFixtures.swift                    # New fixture catalog
├── UITestAssertions.swift                  # New common assertions and accessible text helpers
├── HistoryRobot.swift                      # New history creation/list/order robot
├── ClipboardRobot.swift                    # New pasteboard and capture-state robot
├── RowRobot.swift                          # New copy/pin/delete row-action robot
├── HistoryListUITests.swift                # Refactor to helper-driven scenarios
├── ClipboardAutoCaptureUITests.swift       # Refactor to helper-driven scenarios
├── ClipRowActionsUITests.swift             # Refactor to helper-driven scenarios
└── VisualIdentityUITests.swift             # Refactor to helper-driven scenarios

NextPaste/
└── Production app files                    # Unchanged unless a minimal UI-testing-gated hook is required
```

**Structure Decision**: Keep the existing Xcode project and file-system-synchronized UI test target. Add helper files directly under `NextPasteUITests/` so they are automatically included with the UI test target. Keep reusable launch/window behavior in `UITestAppLauncher.swift` unless duplication reduction requires a small wrapper in `UITestCase`.

## Phase 0 Research Summary

Research decisions are recorded in [research.md](research.md). All technical-context questions are resolved by the clarified specification and current UI test code: Robot pattern plus fixtures/assertions/base setup, UI-test-only production hooks if unavoidable, four-file required scope, hard Sonar duplicate-code gate, and behavior-equivalent parity.

## Phase 1 Design Summary

The test abstraction model is recorded in [data-model.md](data-model.md). Helper and behavior contracts are recorded under [contracts/](contracts/). Validation and run guidance, including UI test and Sonar/manual duplicate checks, are recorded in [quickstart.md](quickstart.md). The managed agent-context plan reference points to this feature plan.

## Complexity Tracking

No constitution violations are present. No complexity exceptions are required.
